response:
to find the denominator we must store the count of prime factors.
***
the number ten is two times five so we store the powers of five.
***
we store the exponent of five in one hundred factorial by summing quotients.
***
the total count of five factors is twenty plus four plus one which equals twenty five.
***
we store the exponent of ten which is fifty in the divisor part.
***
since there are only twenty five fives we store that the fraction remains reducible.
***
after canceling twenty five fives we store that twenty five fives remain in the denominator.
***
the remaining denominator is five raised to the power of twenty five.
***
we store the final result as five to the twenty fifth power for the answer.
***
this value is the denominator of the irreducible fraction we store in our notes.
